opp.ais_why:
• The description and next-step notes reveal a heavily structured RFP: send RFP, Q&A, top-3 selection, vendor selection July 31. The buyer's stated requirements are a centralized cloud-based solution that enforces structured workflows and is ERP-agnostic. There is zero mention of AI, Merlin, Agentic, GenAI, intake or autonomous capabilities in the buyer-stated requirements.
• AI importance is rated 'Don't Know' - the strongest negative indicator. Zycus has no read on buyer AI appetite after multiple RFP cycles. An ERP transition over three years is the dominant driver, not AI. Products include Merlin, Merlin Intake and ANA, but no Agentic AI; the Merlin product line lists ANA, Merlin and Merlin Intake. This is vendor-side scoping with no buyer pull.
• Avoma was fully exhausted - zero calls at opportunity, account or copaair.com domain level despite 10 contacts on file. The process is being run entirely through the RFP document with no recorded vendor calls. With a 'Don't Know' AI rating, an ERP-transition-driven RFP scope and zero AI voice across the detailed timeline, this is Resistant.

metrics.narrative: Copa Airlines articulated the business problem in the Description field and RFP documents: they require a centralized, cloud-based S2P solution to move from manual, fragmented procurement processes to standardized workflows with enhanced visibility, governance, and operational efficiency. The compelling event is a planned ERP transition within three years, requiring an ERP-agnostic platform. However, no quantified metrics were provided—no cost savings target, no cycle-time reduction goal, no error-rate baseline, no headcount impact, and no ROI case documented across all Salesforce fields (Customer_Business_Problem__c, Business_Objectives__c, Value_to_Customer__c, Compelling_Event__c all null) or task email threads. The buyer's April follow-up questions focused on technical module capabilities and a shopping-cart use case for 5,000 crew members (annual uniform selection), signaling operational scope but no financial quantification. The freeze message on 01 Jun explicitly tied the pause to external cost pressures (war, oil prices), confirming cost control is a driver, but the buyer never quantified the procurement inefficiency cost or the expected value of solving it. Without a quantified business case, the deal lacks a defendable ROI anchor to survive macro headwinds or justify budget post-freeze.

competition.narrative: Competition is entirely unknown. Competitors__c and Others_Competitors_Please_specify__c are null, and no competitor names appear in Next_Step__c, Next_Step_History__c, task descriptions, or the Description field. However, the RFP structure proves this is a multi-vendor competitive process: Gartner BuySmart was used for functional-requirements scoring (27 Feb and 03 Mar tasks), a top-3 shortlist was planned (Description timeline: 'Top 3 Selection May 22'), and the process included use-case presentations and a final vendor-selection gate (31 Jul). For a large airline S2P deal of this size ($358.9k) running a formal Gartner-supported RFP, Tier-1 competitors are almost certain: SAP Ariba (strongest in airline/travel vertical), Coupa (Myron's 03 Apr question explicitly referenced Copa's prior 2019 Coupa subscription with 100 users, asking Zycus to price-match Coupa modules—direct evidence Coupa is either incumbent or strong reference point), GEP, and Ivalua are the typical shortlist for enterprise S2P in aerospace/defense. The buyer's question about Coupa pricing on 03 Apr strongly suggests Coupa is either (a) the incumbent being replaced, (b) a competitor in this RFP, or (c) the pricing benchmark Copa is using to evaluate all vendors. Zycus has zero Intel on who else is in the RFP, how competitors are positioned, what the buyer's Gartner scoring showed, or which vendor(s) the buyer preferred before the freeze. The shortlist decision (expected 29 May, never occurred) would have revealed competitors, but that insight is lost. With zero Avoma calls, Zycus never heard the buyer mention a rival, discuss competitive trade-offs, or articulate differentiation preferences. Post-freeze, if a competitor maintained exec-level relationships during the pause (e.g., quarterly CPO or Finance VP check-ins with the relevant stakeholder or Coupa account teams), they will have a significant advantage when the shortlist resumes. The synthesis-map fields (How_are_you_addressing_your_problem_toda__c, Existing_vendor__c, Replacing_What__c, Why_not_Zycus__c, Zycus_Differentiation_Why_Zycus__c) are all null—no current-stack, incumbent, or differentiation Intel captured. AIS_Why__c notes the buyer requirements had 'zero mention of AI, Merlin, Agentic, GenAI', meaning if Zycus pitched Merlin/ANA heavily (Products__c includes Merlin, Merlin Intake, ANA) but competitors offered lower-cost non-AI S2P suites aligned to the buyer's stated ERP-agnostic workflow criteria, Zycus may be overpriced and over-featured for this buyer's appetite. The competition is real, likely strong, and entirely invisible to Zycus.

identify_pain.narrative: Copa Airlines articulated clear operational pain: they are running manual, fragmented procurement processes with poor visibility, weak governance, and operational inefficiency (Description: 'move from manual, fragmented processes to a centralized, cloud-based platform that enhances visibility, governance, and operational efficiency'). The pain owner is the Finance VP, who sponsored the RFP (27 Feb task: 'at the request of the Finance VP'). Specific pain points confirmed: (1) lack of centralized procurement execution (Description: 'centralized, cloud-based solution that streamlines procurement execution'), (2) absence of structured workflow enforcement (Description: 'enforces structured workflows'), (3) integration complexity with existing SAP and future ERP transition risk (Description: 'integrates with the existing SAP landscape' and 'anticipates a potential ERP transition within the next three years; therefore, the selected platform must be ERP-agnostic'), and (4) inability to efficiently handle occasional high-volume user access (Myron's 09 Apr question: 5,000 crew members accessing the system once per year for uniform selection, needing a shopping-cart module with licensing flexibility). However, none of this pain was quantified—no baseline cost, cycle time, error rate, or savings target documented. The freeze message on 01 Jun revealed an additional macro-driven pain: liquidity and cost control under external pressure from war and oil-price spikes, which now overrides S2P modernization urgency. The operational pain is real and confirmed, but the financial pain (cost of inaction) was never quantified, and the macro pain (cash preservation) has now deprioritized the operational pain indefinitely. The pain confirmed is sufficient to justify an RFP, but insufficient to defend budget allocation post-freeze without a quantified ROI case.

items.detail: Deal indefinitely frozen as of 01 Jun 2026 due to external macro conditions (war, oil prices) with no restart timeline. Buyer Myron stated 'all new initiatives are on hold until the war ends and oil prices drop to reasonable levels. I'll keep you posted once we are in a position to acquire the tool.' Zero buyer engagement for 28 days post-freeze. Close date 01 Dec 2026 (155 days away) is entirely unachievable unless initiatives resume within ~30 days and RFP shortlist/vendor selection/negotiation/contracting accelerates dramatically post-restart. High slip risk to Q1 2027 or beyond, or deal may be cancelled if macro conditions persist.

Zero buyer-side Contact Roles, Economic Buyer unidentified, no champion, and decision-making committee entirely unknown. Single-thread risk: only two buyer contacts named in Next Step/task history (Myron who delivered freeze, and task-contact procurement specialist), neither demonstrates champion behaviors or power-role authority. If competitors maintained executive-level relationships during the freeze (e.g. quarterly CFO/CPO check-ins, value-add industry insights), Zycus will be at a significant disadvantage when the shortlist decision resumes. Without an internal advocate to coach Zycus on post-freeze buyer priorities (cost vs functionality trade-off, incumbent replacement risk, AI appetite), win probability is low.

Competition is entirely unknown (Competitors__c empty, no rivals named in Next Step or tasks). The RFP process (Feb-May 2026) was multi-vendor with shortlist expected 29 May 2026, so Zycus likely competed against Tier-1 S2P peers (SAP Ariba, Coupa, GEP, Ivalua are common for large airline deals). Without knowing who Zycus faced in the initial RFP scoring, their pricing/functionality positioning, or which competitor(s) the buyer preferred pre-freeze, Zycus cannot tailor a differentiated re-engagement message once the deal unfreezes. If a competitor stayed engaged during the pause, they may have shifted buyer preference.

The freeze announcement explicitly ties the pause to external macro cost pressures: 'all new initiatives are on hold until the war ends and oil prices drop to reasonable levels' (Myron, 01 Jun 2026). This signals Copa Airlines is prioritizing liquidity preservation and cost control over S2P modernization investment. Even if the macro freeze lifts, the buyer's budget authority and willingness to fund a $358.9k platform deal in a post-freeze environment (likely with tighter CFO oversight and reduced CapEx/OpEx budgets for non-critical projects) is at risk. The Economic Buyer and CFO sign-off process are entirely unknown, so whether budget was ever formally allocated for this RFP is unconfirmed. High risk the deal is cancelled or budget is reallocated post-freeze.

Without ongoing executive-level relationship maintenance, competitors with stronger C-suite relationships (CFO, CPO, CIO) will gain advantage during the pause by staying top-of-mind through value-add touchpoints (industry insights, peer benchmarks, ROI modeling) while Zycus remains silent. Recommend the deal owner's manager (Alexa Bradley, ground truth) reach out to Copa Airlines' procurement leadership (Senior Director Victor Rios or Senior Manager Carlos Velasco, both Account contacts) within 7 days to acknowledge the freeze, express understanding of the macro constraints, and establish a quarterly check-in cadence to monitor restart conditions. This keeps Zycus in the conversation without appearing pushy, and positions for fast re-engagement once initiatives resume.

Map the Economic Buyer and decision-making committee before re-engagement: Zero buyer-side Contact Roles exist on this opp, the Economic Buyer is unidentified (X10a_Sponsor__c empty), and the approval chain (CFO, CPO, CIO, C-level sponsor for a $358.9k S2P platform deal) is entirely unknown. The Next Step log references only 'Myron' (unmatched to any Salesforce Contact, title/role unknown) and task-contact procurement specialist (Negotiations Specialist per Account contact list, not a power role). Without knowing who controls the budget and who will make the shortlist/final decision once the RFP resumes, Zycus cannot tailor a re-engagement message or build an executive-sponsor relationship during the freeze. Recommend using the freeze period to research Copa Airlines' procurement org chart (LinkedIn, ZoomInfo, mutual connections), identify the CPO/CFO/CIO, and have the deal owner (Karson Keogh) or the owner's manager request an intro through the 14 Account-level procurement contacts (Senior Director Victor Rios is the highest-ranking known contact and the likely path to the EB). Add all identified buyer-side stakeholders as Contact Roles on this opp to close the hygiene gap and enable multi-threaded re-engagement.

Build a champion during the freeze to secure insider guidance for the shortlist decision: No champion exists (zero buyer-side Contact Roles, no champion behaviors documented in Next Step or task history). The RFP shortlist decision expected 29 May 2026 never occurred due to the freeze, so Zycus has no insider visibility into how the buyer scored vendors in the initial RFP round (Feb–May 2026) or what will drive the shortlist/final decision once the evaluation resumes. Without a champion to coach Zycus on the buyer's internal priorities (e.g. cost vs functionality trade-off given post-freeze budget constraints, AI/Merlin appetite, technical purchasing depth for fleet/MRO, incumbent replacement risk), Zycus is flying blind. Recommend the deal owner (Karson Keogh) use the freeze period to deepen the relationship with the most engaged procurement contact (task-contact procurement specialist or one of the 14 Account-level contacts, prioritizing Senior Director Victor Rios or Senior Manager Carlos Velasco as exec-level candidates) through value-add outreach: share airline industry S2P benchmarks, invite to a Zycus customer roundtable (peer airline if available), or offer a private Merlin AI demo showcasing post-pandemic cost-optimization use cases. Goal: convert a neutral contact into an active advocate who will push for Zycus internally once the shortlist decision resumes.

value.how_we_win: Coupa is either incumbent or pricing benchmark. Zycus included Merlin/ANA AI capabilities in scope, but buyer requirements mention zero AI language (per AIS_Why__c). If Coupa pitched lower-cost non-AI S2P aligned to buyer's stated ERP-agnostic workflow needs, Zycus may be overpriced. How we win: quantify Merlin ROI in procurement cycle-time and error reduction (not pitched yet), position modular pricing flexibility vs Coupa's user-based model to address buyer's 5k occasional-user licensing concern (09 Apr question), and secure Finance VP or CFO sponsor to validate AI value post-freeze when cost control dominates. Without EB access or champion, cannot execute this wedge.

value.name: SAP Ariba (inferred)
value.source: Inferred from RFP structure: Gartner BuySmart multi-vendor process, airline vertical, SAP integration requirement (Description: 'integrates with the existing SAP landscape'), and typical Tier-1 S2P competitive set for aerospace deals
value.how_we_win: SAP Ariba is strongest in airline/travel vertical and has SAP-native integration advantage (Copa runs SAP today per Description). Buyer also planning ERP transition within three years and requires ERP-agnostic platform, which weakens Ariba's SAP-lock-in positioning. How we win: emphasize Zycus ERP-agnostic architecture as future-proof vs Ariba's SAP dependency, highlight integration flexibility for post-transition ERP (buyer stated this as requirement), and price aggressively on modular model vs Ariba's suite bundling. Risk: Ariba likely has existing Copa relationship through SAP account team and may have maintained exec touch during freeze.
